sourceMovie.title: Memento
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Memento for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Christopher Nolan keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The mystery and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Memento clicked for you, Psycho (1960) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Memento?
faq.answer: Try Psycho, The Girl on the Train, Blue Velvet—each shares DNA with Memento while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Memento?
faq.answer: Memento (2000) is best described as Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Memento worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
